title: Scroll, Bento, Wipe, Magnet & Spring
description: CSS scroll cascade, subgrid bento cards, clip-path word reveals, magnetic buttons, and CSS linear() spring tabs.

drops.title: Scroll-Driven Cascade
drops.description: Pure CSS animation-timeline: view() reveals — cards, timelines, and stats that animate in as they enter the viewport.
drops.concept: Uses the CSS Scroll-driven Animations API with animation-timeline: view() and animation-range to stagger-reveal elements as they scroll into view. Zero JavaScript — all timing is driven by scroll position via the browser's compositor thread. Includes a scroll-progress bar and multiple reveal variants (fade-up, slide-left, zoom-in, perspective flip).
drops.source: MDN Scroll-driven Animations Spec + Codrops Sticky Grid Scroll tutorial

interaction.mouse: Scroll down to trigger each section's cascade animation

drops.title: CSS Subgrid Bento
drops.description: Asymmetric bento dashboard with mouse-tracking glassmorphism highlights using only CSS Grid and backdrop-filter.
drops.concept: A 4-column CSS Grid layout where cards span variable columns and rows for an asymmetric bento aesthetic. Each card gets a mouse-tracking radial glow via CSS custom properties set from JS mousemove. The glassmorphism hover state uses backdrop-filter: blur() and a noise SVG texture overlay for depth.
drops.source: Apple Keynote Bento Grid pattern + CSS Grid Subgrid specification

interaction.mouse: Hover cards to see radial glow follow cursor

drops.title: Clip-Path Word Wipe
drops.description: Four cinematic text reveal variants — translateY overflow wipe, horizontal clip-path reveal, center split, and 3D letter cascade.
drops.concept: Demonstrates four distinct CSS text reveal techniques. Variant A uses overflow:hidden wrappers with translateY to slide words up from below. Variant B uses clip-path: inset() to wipe lines from left. Variant C expands from the center outward via clip-path. Variant D flips individual letters in 3D with rotateX and a spring overshoot. All use IntersectionObserver for scroll-triggered playback.
drops.source: freefrontend.com CSS text animations + veebilehed24.ee modern CSS text effects 2026

interaction.mouse: Scroll to trigger each variant; click ↺ Replay to re-run

drops.title: Magnetic Buttons
drops.description: Eight buttons that elastically attract to the cursor within an 80px radius, with spring-snap return and radial glow.
drops.concept: Each button tracks the global cursor via requestAnimationFrame. Within an 80px pull radius, the button translates toward the cursor proportional to proximity (strength: 0.45). On mouse leave, a cubic-bezier(0.34, 1.56, 0.64, 1) spring transition snaps the button back with a satisfying overshoot. Includes a radial glow that follows the mouse inside the button, and a custom CSS cursor that enlarges on hover.
drops.source: en.inithtml.com Magnetic Hover Effect with Vanilla JavaScript

interaction.mouse: Move near buttons to feel the pull; move away to see spring snap-back
interaction.touch: Touch-only: no magnetic effect (touch lacks hover proximity)

drops.title: CSS Spring Tabs
drops.description: Tab switcher and segmented control with a sliding indicator driven by CSS linear() spring timing — zero JS animation.
drops.concept: The sliding tab indicator moves with left + width CSS transitions that use a linear() timing function encoding a real damped spring decay curve. This produces genuine spring overshoot and settle behavior purely from CSS — no JS animation loop, no WAAPI. Includes two variants: a full tab component (with content panes) and a pill-style segmented control, each with a distinct spring tension.
drops.source: Josh W. Comeau 'Springs and Bounces in Native CSS' + motion.dev spring docs

interaction.mouse: Click any tab or segment to see the spring indicator slide
